first.speaker: The Wanderer
first.text: You walk loud, for someone so small in all this. Sit sometime. The valley likes company more than it lets on.

again.speaker: The Wanderer
again.text: Back again. The dunes moved in the night — you didn't. That's something.

choices.text: The light at the shrine — what is it?
The keeper at the shrine — you know them?

friend.speaker: The Wanderer
friend.text: Ah. You. Good — the evening wanted a second pair of eyes.

choices.text: The light at the shrine — what is it?
The keeper at the shrine — you know them?

who.speaker: The Wanderer
who.text: A wanderer. Names are for people who plan to be found. Ask me again when we know each other.

place.speaker: The Wanderer
place.text: This valley keeps what it's given. Water, footsteps, the patient kind of tree. It kept me. It's started keeping you — I've seen your tracks.

shrine.speaker: The Wanderer
shrine.text: You touched it, then. It hums, doesn't it. Don't ask it questions — it answers in weather, and you don't speak weather yet.

sit_together.speaker: The Wanderer
sit_together.text: They nod at the ground beside them, and say nothing more. It's a good nothing.

choices.text: The light at the shrine — what is it?
The keeper at the shrine — you know them?

about_keeper.speaker: The Wanderer
about_keeper.text: Stubborn as the stone they sweep. We are old arguments, the two of us. Tell them the west path cleared — they'll know what it means.
